name: Causal dynamical triangulations
summary: A non-perturbative lattice approach to quantum gravity in which spacetime is built from elementary 4-simplices respecting causal structure. Numerical simulations of the path integral over causal triangulations show that a 4-dimensional macroscopic spacetime emerges dynamically, with a phase diagram including a 'physical' region resembling de Sitter geometry at large scales.

addresses_puzzles.note: CDT requires a positive cosmological constant in its bare action to produce the de-Sitter-like emergent geometry; this is an input rather than a derivation. Spectral dimension running from 4 (large) to 2 (Planck) is a robust prediction; possible cosmological signatures speculative.

touches_benchmarks.note: Continuous symmetries should emerge in the continuum limit; explicit Planck-scale Lorentz violation not predicted.

introduces.note: Bare Newton's G, bare Lambda, asymmetry parameter alpha between space-like and time-like edges.

history: Ambjorn, Loll (1998) introduced causal triangulations. Ambjorn, Jurkiewicz, Loll (2004), 'Emergence of a 4D world from causal quantum gravity', PRL 93, 131301, arXiv:hep-th/0404156 — demonstrated 4D emergent spacetime numerically. Spectral-dimension running (Ambjorn-Jurkiewicz-Loll 2005, hep-th/0505113) suggested dimensional reduction at Planck scale. Active small-community research with steady numerical progress.

references.citation: Ambjorn, Jurkiewicz, Loll, 'Emergence of a 4D world from causal quantum gravity', Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 131301 (2004)
Loll, 'Quantum gravity from causal dynamical triangulations: a review', Class. Quantum Grav. 37, 013002 (2020)
